fields.description:
## Why
The single `state["signature"]` slot is last-writer-wins, so one kind clobbers another.
This is the root-cause fix and the riskiest slice (back-compat + compaction), done first.
## What
Add `state["attestations"]`, a kind-keyed map applied per-key-LWW like
`process_workflow_run`. `process_signature` writes BOTH `state["attestations"][kind] =
record` AND maintains `state["signature"]` as a LIVE mirror of the MOST-RECENT attestation
(last SIGNATURE event wins) in live compiled state — so the four existing
`state.get("signature")` consumers (`signing.verify_signature`, the close-gate path,
`fsck`/validate, retire_attested_pin) keep working unchanged in this slice, and the
SNAPSHOT/rollback mirror is automatic. Kind is from the SIGNED `manifest[0]` (substring
before first ":"); `data["kind"]` is only a hint, used iff it matches, else ignored; a
blank/unkindable manifest is ignored for routing. Read fold-in: an OLD SNAPSHOT carrying
only `signature` (no `attestations`) is folded into the map on restore. Also add
`state["last_reopened_at"]`: `process_status` records the most recent `closed -> open`
transition timestamp (from pre-update status == "closed" and target == "open"; null if
never), so `compute_validity` can check "not reopened since signed_at" purely from compiled
state. Update `_present.py` `public_state()` with a DEDICATED `attestations` branch that
strips the `signature` (HMAC hex) subkey from each per-kind record (the flat
`_INTERNAL_SUBKEYS` cannot express a wildcard kind level); retain the legacy single-`signature`
strip. 114f owns the canonical per-record shape.
## Scope
`src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py` (`process_signature`, `process_status`,
`process_snapshot` fold-in), `src/rebar/reducer/_present.py`, `src/rebar/reducer/_state.py`
(base-state default), `src/rebar/_commands/compact.py` (mirror is automatic via reduce),
`ticket_state.schema.json`. No change to the signed payload.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `attestations` map added; `process_signature` writes `attestations[kind]` AND keeps the live `state["signature"]` most-recent mirror; routing by authoritative `manifest[0]` (data["kind"] only as validated hint).
- [ ] `state["last_reopened_at"]` records the most recent closed->open timestamp (null if never reopened).
- [ ] Old-snapshot read fold-in: `signature` -> `attestations` on restore; post-snapshot SIGNATURE events replay into the map; new SNAPSHOTs carry both.
- [ ] `public_state()` dedicated `attestations` branch strips the HMAC hex from EVERY per-kind record; legacy single-`signature` strip retained; no hex in show/MCP.
- [ ] Compaction/SNAPSHOT round-trips all kinds; empty `attestations` omitted from `show`/state.
